claims.claim: All four Undangs co-signed and issued declaration naming Tunku Nadzaruddin 12th YDPB
claims.correction: Reporting supports all four Undang met and issued proclamations naming Tunku Nadzaruddin. But no independent inspection of declaration itself or signature page. Safer: 'multiple outlets reported collective participation by all four Undang'.

claims.claim: Four Undangs declared Muhriz deposed, named Nadzaruddin 12th Ruler. Three eligible. One dismissed 341 days earlier.
claims.correction: Four-Undang + Nadzaruddin parts well reported; 341-day arithmetic correct. But 'three were eligible to vote' is stronger than public evidence. No primary source formally stating eligibility in those exact terms.

claims.claim: State government rejects on narrow ground that one Undang (Mubarak) was dismissed 341 days earlier
claims.correction: Mubarak's dismissal was ONE of government's grounds. But rejection was not only framed that way. BERNAMA + Malay Mail say Aminuddin called act inconsistent with Articles 10-12. The Star says Articles 10-13. Calling rejection merely a 'narrow ground' understates what was publicly said.

claims.claim: NS constitution may permit ≥3 Undangs to call upon Ruler to withdraw
claims.source: NS constitution PDF + Malay Mail + Malaysiakini
claims.correction: Official constitution shows Article X deals with 'Abdication or temporary withdrawal by His Highness at the request of the Undangs'. Secondary reporting says ≥3 Undang may act. Exact operative text not independently retrieved. Keep provisional.

claims.claim: Article X(1)(b) UUTKNS 1959 refers to collective Undang action
claims.correction: Official PDF snippets strongly suggest Article X is about withdrawal at request of the Undangs (collective in form). Full text of Article X(1)(b) itself not independently retrieved. Quote the clause directly before publication.

claims.claim: Same Undang who read deposal 19 Apr 2026 proclaimed Muhriz 29 Dec 2008
1967 Undang bypassed Muhriz at 19, electing uncle Tuanku Ja'afar
claims.source: The Edge + mStar + NS royal portal

claims.claim: Mubarak dismissed 13 May 2025 for 33 offences; 341 days before declaration
claims.correction: Dismissal date, 33-offence count, and 341-day arithmetic supported by MB statement. 'Co-signed' is stronger than primary-checked evidence.

claims.claim: DKU presided by Tuanku Muhriz confirmed dismissal 17 Apr 2026
Four Undangs cited alleged royal misconduct from 5 Mar 2026 inquiry
claims.correction: Found only through secondary reporting. No primary inquiry report, declaration text, or primary record establishing 5 Mar 2026 inquiry. Keep unverified.

claims.claim: Telaga Undang speaker Sarifah Norzaidah Al-Qadri 'menderhaka' + referral to religious + police
claims.source: Malay Mail + FMT Bahasa + NST 20 Apr 2026

claims.claim: State government rejected declaration without examining underlying claim
claims.correction: Could not verify independently. Public statements focus on Mubarak's lack of authority and constitutional inconsistency. That's not same as proving government did or did not examine underlying misconduct allegation.

claims.claim: No court ruling or Federal-level constitutional interpretation of Article X threshold has been publicly issued as of 20 April 2026
claims.correction: Absence claim. Not finding a ruling is not proof none exists.

claims.claim: Constitutional provisions at play: Articles 10-12, 16, Clauses 14(3), 24 UUTKNS 1959
claims.source: BERNAMA + Malay Mail + The Star + NS constitution PDF
claims.correction: Supports Article 16, Clause 14(3), Clause 24. But exact constitutional text not independently retrieved. Live discrepancy: Articles 10-12 (BERNAMA/Malay Mail) vs 10-13 (The Star).

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: declaration co-signed and issued by all FOUR Undangs
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Strong reporting that four Undang met and proclamations issued collectively, but no independent inspection of declaration or signature page. 'Co-signed' stronger than primary-checked evidence.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: state government rejects on narrow ground that Mubarak was dismissed 341 days earlier
cardinal_sin_flags.why: One part of government's position, not the whole. Multiple outlets report Aminuddin also said act was inconsistent with specific constitutional articles. 'Narrow' understates government's constitutional argument.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: NS constitution may permit ≥3 Undangs to call upon Ruler to withdraw
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Official PDF snippet confirms Article X is about abdication/temporary withdrawal at request of the Undangs. Could not retrieve full clause text establishing the threshold. Secondary reporting suggests 'at least three' but not primary-verified.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: State government rejected without examining the underlying claim
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Inference about what government did not do. Reporting does not establish that point.
No primary source formally stating three Undang were legally eligible to vote in those terms.
Compresses several legal references into a settled package even though exact text not quoted. Discrepancy across outlets: 10-12 vs 10-13.

omitted_facts: Official NS constitution PDF search results show a heading for 'Abdication or temporary withdrawal by His Highness at the request of the Undangs' and a following Article XA on the YDPB ceasing to be ruler in certain circumstances. If constitutional interpretation is central, the actual text should be quoted directly from this official source.

Live discrepancy in reputable reporting on which articles Aminuddin invoked: BERNAMA + Malay Mail report Articles 10-12; The Star reports Articles 10-13. Should be disclosed rather than silently normalised.

Secondary reporting points to Article 29 as potentially crucial: Malaysiakini and BH-related snippets say decisions of the Undang are valid if carried out by at least three Undang, or as many as remain alive. Competing interpretation too important to omit.

Source-cleanliness issue around Rembau Undang identity: one Malay Mail photo caption still labels Muhamad Shariff Omar; BERNAMA + subsequent reporting establish Abd Rahim Yassin as current Undang of Rembau from 2024, recognized in 2025.
source_assessment: Materially better sourced than v1 on chronology and actors. Still leans too heavily on secondary reporting for hardest legal questions. Could not independently retrieve full text of Article X, Article 29, Clause 14(3), Clause 24 from official constitution. Did not find actual 19 Apr declaration document or court ruling. Live discrepancy on 10-12 vs 10-13.
overall_assessment: v2 fixes biggest problem in v1 by restoring collective-action angle. Chronology of 2008 proclamation, May 2025 dismissal, 17 Apr 2026 DKU sitting, Tunku Nadzaruddin naming all solid. Remaining weaknesses mostly legal and evidentiary: overstates 'narrow' state-govt rejection, asserts constitutional mechanics without direct quotation, makes a few details stronger than primary-checked record supports ('co-signed', 'three were eligible to vote', 'rejected without examining'). Before publication: one more pass with direct quotation from official UUTKNS clauses and ideally the declaration itself.
